expectations.text: Mentions KaTeX as the validation library
expectations.evidence: Response recommends KaTeX explicitly and provides the validate-math.mjs script.

expectations.text: Provides an install command (bun add -g katex or npm install -g katex)
expectations.evidence: Response provides 'bun add -g katex' as the install command.

expectations.text: Provides or references a Node.js/JavaScript script that validates all three block types
expectations.evidence: Response references the bundled validate-math.mjs script handling all three block types.

expectations.text: Mentions that the solution exits with code 1 on errors (CI-friendly)
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly states 'exits with code 1 if any errors (CI-friendly)'.

expectations.text: Notes the important caveat that KaTeX passing does NOT guarantee GitHub renders correctly
expectations.evidence: Response has a dedicated 'Critical caveat' section about GitHub pre-processor stripping \\ inside $$ blocks.
